title: The Fermentation Master
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In 1972 Jeonju, South Korea, an elderly fermentation master races to complete a secret archive of her family's eighty-year recipes before a government inspector arrives to decide her studio's fate.
long_description:
Park Soonja, 67, has run her family's doenjang and kimchi fermentation studio in Jeonju since burying her husband in the Korean War, keeping alive a practice that stretches back to 1890, anchored by forty-two clay onggi jars and salt harvested from the Gochang tidal flats. When South Korea's food industry begins mass-producing fermented goods by chemical shortcut, a provincial hygiene official arrives to assess whether the old studio meets modern standards. Her apprentice Jinho wants stainless steel vats. Her daughter Miyeon wants her to accept the food company's acquisition offer. Neither of them knows that for seven years Soonja has been writing a secret ledger — ninety-two fermentation formulas, annotated with everything that cannot be captured in a formula alone: the reading of temperature by hand, the mineral distinction between a salt harvest's surface layer and its depth, the mountain doenjang her neighbor's mother made before the wars.

Set across twenty-three days in October 1972, the novel follows the inspection preparation, the family pressure, and the slow revelation of what Soonja has actually been doing, as it becomes clear that she never intended to sell, never intended to close, and never intended the ledger for commerce — only for the National Folklore Archive in Seoul, so that what she knows will not die with her. A novel about craft, about what cannot be transmitted except through long attention, and about the forms that preservation takes when institutions are not yet ready to value what they cannot measure.
main_characters: Park Soonja, 67, the fermentation master and keeper of the studio
Jinho, 24, her apprentice, eager and technically minded
Park Miyeon, 41, Soonja's daughter, a schoolteacher and pragmatist
Oh Jungsik, 44, the provincial hygiene official
Elder Kim, 78, a neighboring grandmother and repository of older knowledge
Baek Seunghyun, 35, a food company representative
Dohee, 28, Miyeon's daughter and Soonja's granddaughter, an art teacher
